summary: Cathy O'Neil briefly announces the creation of the Simons Center for Data Analysis and the appointment of Leslie Greengard as its director. She notes that despite Greengard's distinguished reputation in mathematics and computer science, she hadn't heard of this new center until seeing the announcement. The post is essentially a call to readers for more information, as she found only a minimal description of the center's mission to analyze large-scale data sets and develop new mathematical methods.
key_points: The Simons Foundation has established a new Center for Data Analysis
Leslie Greengard, a distinguished mathematician and computer scientist, was named its director
The center's stated mission is analyzing large-scale data and developing innovative mathematical methods
Even well-connected data scientists like O'Neil were unaware of the center's existence
money_quotes: Has anyone heard of the new Simons Center for Data Analysis?
Neither had I until just now.
Please inform me if you know more about this center. I got nothing.
key_insight: Major new data science initiatives can emerge with little fanfare even in well-connected mathematical and scientific communities.
